RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat at his best; held in second when odds-on for hurdling debut at Hexham (2m, good) but he built on that when trouncing two rivals at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last week; could have a lot more to offer in this sphere and he's respected under a penalty.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made it 2-9 on the Flat when landing a Brighton handicap (1m2f, good to firm) last Sunday; that was a Class 6 event but he's a half-brother to four hurdle winners and should have a future at this new discipline; needs a close look in market.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Big prices and he's hasn't shown a great deal in four Flat runs since December including a Southwell handicap (7f, AW); best watched on hurdling debut.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won three in a row on AW (1m2f/1m4f) in February and he was a creditable second when upped to 1m6f in a Redcar handicap (good to firm) last month; takes on two previous winners on this hurdling debut but he has some fair form on the Flat and he's not ruled out.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to winners Alpine Oasis (1m/1m4f AW; RPR 84), Albion Princess (1m-10.6f; 74), Lillistar (2m AW; 63) and Showstorm (German 9.5f); dam 1m1f-1m6f winner (89), out of Listed-placed 1m winner; stablemate of Tiny Riot and market guide on her debut.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Started a short price on his Brighton debut in a weak-looking maiden one month ago, when a remote last of five; everything went wrong that day, with him being green and ill at ease round the undulations, and he's obviously felt capable of better.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gelded half-brother to two 6f winners for the yard (out of a 6f winner), one who went close on debut; trainer 4-24 with 2yos on this track; worth a market check.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Started a big price when well held in a Newbury novice recently; trainer a pleasing 6-25 with 2yos here down the seasons but this one will need a big step forward.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 170,000 breeze-up purchase who built on a promising debut when third at Lingfield to two who went on to run well at Royal Ascot last time; rates the main threat to This Moment.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well-related 120,000gns yearling who finished a good way off the front pair when easy to back on his debut at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) this month; needs improvement.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Early-foaled 130,000euros yearling, 100,000euros breeze-up 2yo; stable just getting going with juveniles for the season (had a newcomer win at Ripon recently) and the market will show what's expected.

RaceName: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Plenty to like about his debut second at Hamilton, when well clear of the rest having run green early; ran to a similar level at Chester one month ago and sets the standard on either effort.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed narrowly at Ripon (1m2f, good) last time, recording first win since 2024; no spring chicken, being an 8yo, but he's technically still unexposed over this trip and remains well treated on historical data; should remain competitive.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Stuck his neck in front in the final strides over C&D (good) on seasonal debut, ending a three-year losing spell; still well handicapped on peak form and commands respect under very promising young amateur Henry Callan, who has built up an impressive strike-rate.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who has been ridden by Rebecca Hitchen since joining current yard, posting form figures of 31216; hindered by wide trip in AW contest on reappearance most recently; could go well with that outing under his belt.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Campaigned mostly at up to 8.5f and has seemed ideally suited by Beverley/Newcastle, having registered all of his top-two finishes at those venues; doesn't look the answer in this scenario.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Has largely respectable form this term, most recently fifth to Shimmering Sands over C&D; weighted to get closer to that rival on the revised terms and is on an attractive mark; still seeking first handicap win away from Beverley but can't be dismissed.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Had wind surgery (his third) since last run; form at Pontefract features a success last August (off 2lb higher; one of three wins under Charli Parker) and best effort this season; record of 0-19 when racing above Class 6 is the negative.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Lxvi And P Demilow
Spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo whose form since handicapping features a reappearance win at Redcar (1m) in April and keeping-on second at Carlisle (1m1f) last time; may still have further progress in him and this return to 1m2f is worth a go; shortlisted.

RaceName: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Has performed respectably in last two attempts under Evie Lee, finishing third both times, including latest start; however, continues on a long losing run, partly exemplified by being 0-14 for current stable.

RaceName: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: All of his four hurdle wins have been at Stratford and latest was a front-running success over about 2m3f (good to firm) last month; remains feasibly treated off 3lb higher and this in-form 7yo is a major player on this drop back in trip.

RaceName: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran surprisingly well behind the classy Murcia in a mares' hurdle at Listowel (2m, good) last month but she struggled back in handicap chase last time; now returns to hurdling but she's hard to predict and her last win was in October 2024; others are more convincing.

RaceName: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returned after six months off with a win in a Warwick handicap (2m, good) last November; looks interesting on that form but he fell on his final run during the winter and was tailed off on his Aintree reappearance last month; still has low mileage but he needs to bounce back near best.

RaceName: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a handicap hurdle at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) last August but he's been out of sorts in three runs (chase/hurdle) this spring and overall record is now 1-31; opposable.

RaceName: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 15-race maiden but he ran well in two hurdle handicaps last month including when runner-up over C&D (good to firm) on his penultimate start; only 1lb higher here and he should go well again.

RaceName: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who was runner-up in a Worcester handicap (2m, good) last month and he had an excuse when upped to 2m4f there six days later; could resume his progress on this drop back in trip and he's an interesting contender for Dan Skelton.

RaceName: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap
OwnerName: Int Plywood (lmporters) Ltd And C Mason
Spotlight: Went close second time out as a 3yo and won on his second start last season, which bodes well off the back of a pleasing Windsor comeback five weeks ago; needs considering at this lower level off a career-low mark.

RaceName: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap
Spotlight: Tumbled down the weights for three yards on the AW and took advantage of a lenient turf mark for his new trainer, without the usual headgear, under Myla Coppins at Windsor last Monday; had a good bit in hand there and has no penalty for it; obvious chance.

RaceName: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap
Spotlight: Fair turf debut in April, when third to Tickettothestars (5lb better off today), but didn't back it up last time; did get in a bumping match early, though, and it's too soon to write her off.

RaceName: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap
Spotlight: No excuses off her revised mark since a Nottingham win off 4lb lower (well drawn at Lingfield; some way below that level at Bath latest); completely exposed now.

RaceName: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap
Spotlight: Fair turf/handicap debut on his return from six months off on first run since being gelded; entitled to have come on for that and it's still early days with him.

RaceName: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who was all the rage for a Bath 0-50 classified event for her new yard early in the month, despite having a bit to find at the weights; didn't appear to have any excuses there but is better off back into handicap company.

RaceName: Danny O'Neill Birthday "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than Two Runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Unraced for Godolphin; won going away, looking a useful sort, in good-ground bumper at Down Royal in sole start for Anthony McCann; sold for £80,000 later in May; this son of Dubawi is an interesting recruit who could do well on the Flat for his new yard.

RaceName: Danny O'Neill Birthday "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than Two Runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Decorated Knight half-brother to three winners, notably Golden Wolf (1m2f; RPR 96); dam maiden (68), out of unraced sister to top stayer Yeats; makes belated debut aged six; probably best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Danny O'Neill Birthday "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than Two Runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 50,000euros yearling; by Camelot; sibling to five winners, notably Luxembourg (1m-1m4f including Group 1; RPR 124); dam maiden (71); stablemate of once-raced Sea And Sun; respected newcomer, especially if the market speaks positively.

RaceName: Danny O'Neill Birthday "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than Two Runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Brings the worst form among the runners who have racecourse experience, having trailed home last of 11 in 1m2f maiden at Newbury; however, may post a better effort in this weaker race with the outing under his belt.

RaceName: Danny O'Neill Birthday "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than Two Runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Stablemate of newcomer Camelot Champion; showed some promise with second of seven in 1m3f event at Ripon in April; ran to an ordinary RPR, having finished 11l behind the odds-on winner who set the standard, but looks open to improvement.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Record of 1-14 over fences but that win came at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) in April and he was a front-running second behind a major improver at Southwell (2m, good) last time; this is a different test but his hurdles win was over 2m4f and he still looks feasibly treated; interesting contender.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but she's been a big improver since switched to chasing and completed a double with an emphatic win at Fontwell (2m3f, good) 19 days ago; now 11lb higher but she's on a roll and could continue on an upward curve over fences; key player.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who has reached the frame in all of his four chase runs; was no match for A Little Something when runner-up at Fontwell (2m3f, good) this month but that was after an absence and he has a big swing in the weights with that rival; could be dangerous.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden who has form figures of 0FP706 over hurdles and was a remote fifth at a big prices on his recent chase debut at Worcester (2m4f, good); has a lot to prove and he needs a tongue-tie to make a big difference.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Ran respectably when fourth over hurdles at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; unexposed over fences but he remains a maiden after 17 starts under rules and needs to find more back in this sphere; had wind surgery since his last run.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Triple point winner who scored in that sphere on her penultimate run; now switches to regulation fences but she's 0-12 over hurdles and this looks tough from out of the weights.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Has never run on ground faster than good (and taken out before on account of unsuitable good ground), so conditions could be a worry; hard to place after a Listed win in Italy last autumn ruined his mark and has struggled in three runs for this yard.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive last year and as good as ever when third on his Ascot comeback in the spring; pulled hard in first-time cheekpieces (removed today) last time, an effort that looks best ignored, and he's one of the likelier winners back from a short break.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Done his winning on the AW but is fine on turf; not best drawn when behind All Ways Glamorous at Ascot in the spring (now 2lb worse off) before not looking to see out 6f last time; needs a career-best off his current mark but this is less competitive.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Wins have come over 6f on Tapeta, latterly off 6lb lower; now has her first 5f run since beaten at odds-on on her 2yo debut but she showed good toe to make most round Chester last time before picked off by two who'd sat in the rear and this mark looks within range.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Held since winning a couple of nurseries on Tapeta last backend, latterly off 3lb lower; looked to find the sixth furlong beyond him last time but he has to prove this mark is within range on turf.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 4yo filly who finished fourth of six in 1m event at Ripon where she was the only newcomer in the field; ran to an ordinary RPR but may do better with the outing/experience under her belt.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced 4yo who brings the worst form having registered RPRs in the 30s; handicaps should be more suitable shortly.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has made the frame in all four outings and the form stacks up well; good second, with a few subsequent winners behind her, in Nottingham handicap (1m) most recently; subsequently withdrawn from Listed event due to soft ground; sets a rock-solid standard.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Never dangerous but showed a bit of promise in making some late headway in 1m contest at Haydock, finishing sixth of eight; may take a step forward with the experience under her belt.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Related to several winners for her connections; has made the frame in 1m2f contests at Newbury and Salisbury, while shaping as if this drop in distance is likely to suit; addition of tongue-tie may also contribute to a materialisation of progress; one of the main players.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles in Ireland but he finished third of 20 in a Cork handicap (2m, yielding) in April on his final run; left Shark Hanlon for 24,000euros since; now tries a new trip after a break but he needs a close look on his first run Dan Skelton.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt over hurdles when justifying support at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) 17 days ago; up 7lb but that was an emphatic success and she's open to more progress; big player.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Struggled at massive prices in three hurdles runs last year and he unseated when favourite in a point on his return last month; still early days but he needs a transformation on this switch to a handicap.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well held in his first three hurdle runs before being pulled up on handicap debut at Bangor (2m3f, good) last month; that was after an absence but he needs cheekpieces to make a big difference on this step back up in trip.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has finished down the field in her five handicaps and was 13l behind Slack Alice at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) last time; passed over.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner (1m6f-2m) but he's been well held in four hurdle runs since April including a Worcester handicap (2m, good; 33-1); this stiffer stamina test could help but he needs to show a lot more.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well held in his first two runs and was pulled up in a maiden at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) on his recent return; now goes handicapping but he needs improvement to make an impact from out of the weights.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Wins for Michael Dods came on soft (2yo debut) and good to soft, and he hung left both runs on good to firm; 4lb higher than his last winning mark and returns from ten months off, having been picked up for 1,000gns; hard to find many positives.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Hit traffic at Windsor (first-time cheekpieces) last month and built on that back down to 7f last time; been put up 3lb for it but this is less competitive and he's high on the shortlist.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Free-going gelding who was pestered in front and didn't see out 8.7f on Tapeta last time, having previously won easily (7f) off 9lb lower; drop back in trip will suit on his turf debut.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Half-sister to five turf winners who bettered her AW efforts when second at a big price at Windsor (1m) last time; didn't look to fail through lack of stamina but it gave something to build on.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Inclined to hang left a couple of times, including when well beaten on his turf debut/comeback in the spring; since undergone wind surgery and the market should give some clues as to what's been made of his opening mark.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who faded late on his first run at beyond 7f when behind Flyta at Windsor a fortnight ago; much will hinge on how he responds to first-time headgear down another 3lb.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Useful 2yo for Charles Hills who proved hard to place last year and tumbled down the weights; found his level again now, just cracking late over 1m last time, and won't mind the return to turf.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Well treated on the form he showed last August, including win at Haydock and close third at the York Ebor festival; absent since finishing rather tamely over C&D two months ago.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 4-9 in 1m events on turf, most recently scoring at Salisbury on penultimate start; well held in competitive 1m2f event latest; drop back to ideal trip is the angle with him.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden winner for previous yard; not particularly solid on his handicap form this term for new stable (40-1 last time) but is the type to bear in mind if there's market support.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: 4yo filly whose form at Pontefract comprises her sole turf win, gained over 6f in 2024, and strong-finishing second (nearly got up) over 1m last time; major player back up just 1lb.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner; best form this term when placed a few times in spring, showing that he retains plenty of ability aged 11; gained last win off 7lb higher over C&D and can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-time winner; has come up well short in big fields at York a couple of times of late; otherwise a consistent sort and has frame possibilities off current mark in this scenario.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: 12-time winner; creditable second in this race in 2022 and 2025 but on both occasions he was in better form at the time; out of sorts since his last success.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Front-runner who returned from seven-month break with 7l win in first-time visor over C&D (good to firm) last month; only 3lb higher than for that success but he failed to repeat the form when a remote fifth at Worcester (2m7f, good); that was another blip in his patchy profile and he needs to bounce back again.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made it 4-15 over fences when forging clear to win by 9l at Fakenham (2m5f, good) last month; that was in a small field but he hit a personal best with that success and a 4lb rise looks fair enough; respected.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Point and dual hurdle winner who made 2-7 in fences when landing the odds in a mares' handicap at Worcester (2m4f, good; tongue-tie added) 23 days ago; jumped well under a positive ride in that race and she's a big player off only 2lb higher here.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 12yo who recorded his tenth point when scoring on his comeback at Kingston Blount (3m, good) last month; this is his only his third run under rules but his first two resulted in a win and second in hunter chases and latest was over C&D; could go well back in this sphere and he's not ruled out on handicap debut.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Blinkers did the trick when winning a Tapeta handicap for Roger Varian last autumn and he made it 2-2 in headgear off 3lb lower over C&D (good to soft; cheekpieces) early in the month; been taken out on account of good to firm going before; player otherwise.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Looked to find this trip beyond him last month but worth another try over it on latest Chepstow evidence (7f, soft); on a workable mark again now, having missed a lot of last year for Ralph Beckett.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Tended to stick to slow turf/Polytrack; missed a year after finishing lame last June and was entitled to have needed his comeback, when third to Grey Soul over C&D; hit traffic late that day and is 2lb better off, so there's a fair chance he turns things round.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who turned in a solid handicap debut in the spring before being drawn wide down to 7f at York; touch disappointing last time but this is less competitive back up in trip.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Won three over this trip last year, twice from the front on turf, latterly off a 3lb lower mark; well held in two runs back but this is less competitive under Billy Loughnane and he may well be able to get his own way in this smaller field; possibilities if so.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Clearly happier on turf than the AW but is handicapped accordingly and was in no kind of form in the spring; the returning cheekpieces need to induce a revival.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
OwnerName: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr
Spotlight: Scored narrowly off 1lb lower over C&D when completing an April double; unable to sustain a good start to the campaign, having shown a dip in form the last twice.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
Spotlight: Attractively treated, having gained all wins off current mark or higher; has rather lacked consistency this year but holds a good chance provided he turns up in top form.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who has proved largely consistent and is knocking on the door, having posted form figures of 222 since switched to turf; ran well in 3yo handicap over C&D (good) most recently; solid claims.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed at Beverley (good) last time, recording a first win away from Catterick and first over 5f; proved suited by the belated drop back in distance and may remain competitive; close second in this race in 2023.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
Spotlight: Latest effort suggests he remains in form; still on a long losing sequence but looked an unlucky third, having been forced to switch, over C&D on penultimate start; possibilities off same mark.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
Spotlight: Similar type to Queen Sana, being a 3yo maiden who has proved consistent in the main; interesting on penultimate effort, which has been strongly franked by the improving winner since.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
OwnerName: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt
Spotlight: Seems ideally suited by Catterick, the scene of his April win and bronze medal latest start; has an unconvincing record at Pontefract; new headgear needs to make a difference.

RaceName: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap
Spotlight: Not quite in top form this term, beaten favourite at Thirsk most recently; went close over C&D last autumn but her ideal level is Class 6, as a record of 0-18 in higher grades reflects.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Completed a double over hurdles at Fakenham (2m, good soft) last October and she's done well back on the Flat this spring including a win on firm ground at Bath (11.6f); her last hurdle win was off 6lb lower but that was a comfortable success and she's in the mix back in this sphere.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdles winner who returned to form with a second at Warwick (2m, good) 26 days ago, and that was behind a major improver who has won seven in a row since the end of May; on same mark as for that last run and she's a big player if she can back that up.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Not quite at her best this year but she was placed at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) on her penultimate run and is now 6lb lower than for her win at Uttoxeter last summer; could go well back in trip and she's not ruled out.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Hale Racing, Lyle Farming Company & Ptnr
Spotlight: Useful Flat handicapper who showed promise in maiden hurdles but she was beaten 28l in a Uttoxeter handicap (2m, good) last month; still early days in this sphere but she has bit to prove after that last run; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who won at Leicester (1m7f, soft) last autumn and she bounced back with a close call in a Kempton handicap (2m, good; sustained a wound to her left fore) in March; there was little separating the first three last time but she's on same mark here and is key player on her return.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed promise in maiden hurdles last summer and she ran well for a long way before falling at the last on her reappearance at Fakenham (2m, good; first run after wind surgery) last month; still unexposed after only six starts and she needs watching in market on handicap debut.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Her last win was in a Plumpton maiden in November 2023; mixed results over hurdles this year but was runner-up at Plumpton (2m, soft) in February and she finished a fair fourth at Fontwell (2m3f, good) 19 days ago; continues to edge down the weights and she could be in the mix back in trip.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Turned things around when scoring at Wincanton (1m7f, good) in April and she followed up under a penalty Taunton (2m, good); came up a bit short in hat-trick bid over C&D (good to firm) last month but that was another good effort and she still looks on a workable mark; shortlisted.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten 21l or more in all five runs including a Huntingdon handicap (2m, good to firm) on her reappearance last month; still unexposed but she needs improvement to get involved.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Third in a Sligo handicap (2m2f, good) last August but she's not gone on from that and has been pulled up and tailed off in last two starts; overall record is now 0-17 and others are preferred,

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sole hurdle win was in a Taunton novice (2m, good to firm) in November 2024; generally struggled last year but she reappeared with an encouraging fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) this month; should get a decent tow into this race and has each-way claims if she can build on that last run.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up four times last year but she reappeared with a tailed-off effort over C&D (good) 13 days ago and is now 0-24 over hurdles; others are more convincing.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdle and dual chase winner when trained by Seamus Mullins; flopped over fences (pulled up) on stable debut in March and was well held back over hurdles at Taunton last time; well handicapped on her best Irish form but she needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Tailed off at massive prices in three novice/maiden hurdles since March; now goes handicapping but she makes no appeal from a long way out of the weights.

RaceName: Plan A Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Latest effort at Doncaster can be ignored, with him hanging badly right early in the straight and ending up on the stand rail; always the concern that he's tackling another left-handed course, though, while it's possible he was flattered from the front on debut.

RaceName: Plan A Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner
Spotlight: Steady progression on the AW, latterly when one of three to come away (8.7f; third a winner since); goes up in trip on his turf debut and sets the standard under Billy Loughnane.

RaceName: Plan A Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well supported for debut in a Newbury maiden that's worked out well but needed the experience, pulling hard early and lugging left before finishing nicely; will have learned plenty from that and holds leading claims.

RaceName: Plan A Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to the yard's 1m2f Royal Ascot winner (good to firm) Quai De Bethune; may not have appreciated very soft ground on his debut early in the month and market useful for last year's successful trainer.

RaceName: Plan A Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not without hope in two runs in the spring for a yard that's had a couple of winners lately; will need a good step forward to take this, though, and is likely to be seen to better effect once handicapping.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Still a maiden and balance of form this season doesn't strongly suggest he'll defy top weight in this field; on a positive note, he's nicely treated on peak 3yo efforts.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Losing run is mounting up but he shaped well from the front over C&D (good) eight days ago, eventually tying up having kicked clear too soon in hindsight, beaten only 2l; big chance off same mark with Ethan Tindall taking off a bonus 5lb, provided his energy is used better.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden who has inconsistent form but posted one of his better efforts last time; sole attempt over C&D (on debut) resulted in his peak form; interesting back here.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Has done her winning over 5f, most recently off 4lb higher at Pontefract last September (inconsistent since); attractively treated but has something to prove back up in trip.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo maiden who failed to settle when back over 7f most recently; performed encouragingly in last two 6f attempts, making the frame at Newcastle (AW) and Haydock; could go well.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo maiden who has broadly respectable 7f form this term; gives the impression this return to 6f is worth exploring; possibilities off a mark that's 7lb below his opening one.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Has made the frame twice since dropped back to 5f, both times shaping as if this return to 6f is likely to suit; indeed, she gained most recent win over this trip; close fourth at Pontefract latest; in the mix.

RaceName: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Modest 3yo who has a peak RPR of 48; managed to open his account in Carlisle event (6f) on penultimate start but wasn't in the same form last time; seems to have limitations.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Triple-figure prices for three runs on the AW (8.7f-1m3f), finishing last of four when last seen in April; couldn't be considered on her turf/handicap debut unless there's good support this time.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Won three on the AW earlier in the year; often ran well here last summer but off much lower marks and it may well be that the handicapper has closed in now.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Likes it here and took advantage of the drop to 0-50 classified company when winning a weak race over C&D early in the month; that won't have hurt his confidence but this is a deal more competitive.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
OwnerName: J O'Mulloy And W R Muir
Spotlight: 1m2f AW nursery winner last autumn who made a subsequent winner work for it on his first run since being gelded, and after a break, at Leicester three weeks ago; longer trip looks ideal and his claims are obvious off the same mark for a yard that does well here.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: This gelding still looked raw when last of five on his recent handicap debut at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm; 7-1), having been gelded; could well have more to offer down in class though.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Half-brother to the same connections' useful stayer Kyle Of Lochalsh; blew the start last time but starts handicap life at a lowly level and should be suited by today's stiffer test; better can be expected.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Two expensive defeats since switched to turf, looking to have no excuses upped to this trip last time having bumped into one at Redcar in April; questions to answer going in a first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces combination.

RaceName: Dress For The Occasions Handicap
Spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in three runs over 1m last backend, since when he's been gelded; well related (half-brother to useful stayer Live Your Dream) and in good hands, but would want to see some support after eight months off.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed by 1l last time to complete a Beverley double; back in good form but she failed in two previous hat-trick attempts and was beaten off 2lb lower (finished fourth) in this race 12 months ago.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-time winner; kept on for never-dangerous fifth, beaten under 3l, when favourite for the latest Beverley race won by Anificas Beauty; closely matched with that rival on the revised terms.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: D J Jones & M T Brougham
Spotlight: Scored off lower marks on turf and AW last term; inconsistent this season but is interesting on his penultimate effort, namely third at Ripon behind a pair of subsequent scorers.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: S & S Kyriakides, K Mount & F Reay
Spotlight: Gave impression something was possibly amiss last time; remains a longstanding maiden but his resurgent May efforts (finished third then close second) suggested a belated first win may be near.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Novice winner who is 0-12 in handicaps but has shown positive signs with second-place finishes at Beverley (behind Anificas Beauty on worse terms) and Hamilton the last twice, despite taking a good hold both times; possibilities, especially if settling better.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Placed in sole 1m attempt, raced over shorter otherwise; ran respectably last time in a Leicester classified event he'd landed 12 months earlier; hasn't won a handicap since 2023.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Ran respectably in this contest last year (finished fifth) and at Beverley latest start; however, this three-time Newcastle AW winner is only 1-26 on turf, the success registered in 2023.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo filly who initially improved for the fitting of a visor, dead-heating at Wetherby then nose second at Bath; has regressed in two starts since but may stage a turnaround with the headgear, which has evidently stopped working, now removed.

RaceName: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner for previous yard; has achieved little on turf, latterly in two starts for new stable; enough to prove.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Couldn't go overboard about the form of his win at Brighton (6f, good to firm) last month; he did it well enough and today's extra distance should suit but he's likely to be vulnerable under a penalty all the same.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 20,000gns yearling;  fourth foal; half-brother to French 10.5f winner Galimatia; dam French 9.7f winner, sister to US 1m2f winner Galawi, half-sister to 1m3f/1m4f winner Galikeo, out of 1m4f Group 1 winner; looks the part on paper but yard's 2yos yet to hit the target in 2026 (0-7; all unplaced).

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
OwnerName: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar
Spotlight: 37,000euros yearling; second foal; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 70), half-sister to winners Breathtaking Look (6f/7f Group 3) and Super Lover (Spanish 8.5f-10.5f); stable had a winning 2yo newcomer recently (well backed); check betting.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed some promise in his first two starts (5f, soft and good), after which he was gelded; not so good when favourite at Ripon 11 days ago (6f, good); hopes rest on the extra furlong, plus AW, sparking something extra.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 13th foal; half-brother to six winners including Fox Legacy (1m1f/1m2f; RPR 118), Ceiling Kitty (5f 2yo including Group 2; 104) and Possible Man (1m-1m2f including 2yo/AW; 102); dam placed at 6f-1m (including 2yo/AW; 88), half-sister to Japanese 1m Grade 1 winner Jeweler; already gelded; stable's 2yos are ticking along nicely and he's an interesting contender.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Won a 7f seller at Leicester (good to firm; 11-8 favourite) on his debut five weeks ago despite doing plenty wrong; this will demand more of him but he should be more streetwise with the run behind him.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Attracted support at big prices on debut at Nottingham (6f, good) four weeks ago but finished tailed off; reportedly unsuited by the undulations there but connections have wasted no time deploying headgear.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 9-2, made some late headway into third of nine on debut at Leicester (7f, good) 16 days ago; improvement essential but also quite likely; one to consider on this switch to AW.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 40-1 and always behind in a 5.7f maiden at Bath (good) 24 days ago; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 11th foal; half-sister to seven winners including Firmament (7f/1m including 2yo/AW; RPR 115), Seven Heavens (5f-7f including 2yo/Hong Kong; 107), You're Hired (7f-11.4f including AW; 103) and Romanticizing (1m; 96); dam 7f-1m1f winner (including Group 3; 115); stable have had a couple of 2yos win first time out this year and she's a major player.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 25-1 and little short-term promise when eighth in a Bath maiden (5.7f, good) 24 days ago.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten in his two bumpers and a 1m2f Windsor novice.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fair, four-race maiden on AW Flat for Jamie Osborne last year; runner-up in two of his eight hurdle races for Warren Greatrex but soundly beaten on last two outings, claimed for £6,000 from a seller in May on latest; best watched for now.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Tailed off in a maiden at Lingfield (7f, good) and novice here (1m2f, good to firm); hooded in both but not today.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fair hurdles form to win four in a row (2m2f-2m4f) last summer but lost his form in that sphere; debut under Flat rules when 17l sixth of ten in maiden at Goodwood (1m6f, good; 18-1) ten days ago, making most, which is something to build on but his turn on the Flat may not come just yet.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 90,000gns foal, 34,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Motazzen (10.5f-1m6f Flat, RPR 90; 2m-2m5f hurdle) and Crystal Mer (2m/2m4f hurdle); dam French 1m3f and 1m4f (Listed) winner (107), half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Matterhorn; faces notable opposition on his debut but needs a market check.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Better here on second start (1m, good to firm) but still well held and modest form; sold out of Roger Varian's for 17,500gns three days later and has been gelded; best watched for now.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Well-bred Frankel colt who was ninth of ten at Newbury (16-1) but kept on well when fourth of six in maiden at Sandown (again 1m2f on good; 15-2) 16 days ago; this longer trip should suit him well, so further improvement (which is needed) is on its way.

RaceName: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Similar fair form when beaten a neck in maiden at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) and third of five in novice at Thirsk (1m4f, good); good place chance but probably needs extra if she's to win this.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: All runs at 6f; improved at the third attempt when winning at Wolverhampton 13 days ago, keeping on well; 7f should suit and he has been found an uncompetitive race in which to try to defy a penalty.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 25,000gns foal, 27,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 7f AW/1m2f turf winner Titainium (RPR 77); dam unraced half-sister to winners Delius (Australian 1m1f/1m2f Group 1) and Nkosikazi (1m2f Group 3); likely best watched.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Makes some appeal on paper and he was 11-2 for his debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) 13 days ago; caught a bit wide there and ran with more encouragement than the bare figures might suggest; possible improver.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 25,000gns foal; fourth foal; brother to 1m4f AW winner Krona (RPR 80) out of a 1m2f winner (69); likely one for more time and distance.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to winners Bullet Point (1m including AW; RPR 111), Pastiche (7f including AW 2yo; 96) and El Salvaje (1m2f AW; 77); dam 5f 2yo/7f winner (including Listed; 102); apprentice ridden and stable not known for 2yo success but he does have a useful pedigree.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Big prices and yet to beat a rival in two runs over sprint trips (gelded before latest).

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Poor form in two sprint runs this summer; new trip/surface not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 16-1 and no real threat in a maiden at Leicester (7f, good) 16 days ago; needs to leave that well behind but she is bred to do so at some point.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: First foal of a 6f 2yo/7f AW winner; stable's juveniles 0-17 this year; likely best watched.

RaceName: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Big prices and poor form in two 6f runs this summer; no appeal.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Mr And Mrs R Everist And Partner
Spotlight: 12-1 for both starts, when third of seven at Lingfield (6f, AW) but well beaten at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) three weeks later; stiff task.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 100,000gns yearling who looks bred for sprinting; 18-1, just over 4l third of eight in maiden at Leicester (6f, good to firm) five weeks ago was fair form; it sets the standard in this field and he should improve.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 72,000euros yearling by Blackbeard; going down by nearly 9l in C&D maiden (good to firm; 18-1) last Monday was low-level form, but he should have benefited from the experience.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 8,000euros yearling, resold £35,000 yearling by Dubawi Legend; second foal; dam unplaced at 5f-6f (RPR 45), closely related to a US 8.5f stakes winner, out of 6f-winning half-sister to prolific 5f-7f winners Whitbarrow and Green Park; major yard runs two and he needs a close look.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: James Dellaway And Jon & Kay Collins
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; third foal; brother to US 5.5f/7f turf winner Chardy Party (including Grade 3; RPR 103), half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Sunny Smile (79); dam 6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 103); early days this term for the yard's 2yos but one was beaten a nose three weeks ago on second outing.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling by Showcasing; first foal; dam 1m winner (RPR 65), half-sister to triple 5f Group 1 winner Bradsell, out of 7f 2yo Listed winner; his trainer's first 2yo runner this term and he hasn't had a 2yo winner on turf in recent seasons.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 25-1, beaten nearly 10l when seventh of nine in novice at Sandown (5f, good) 17 days ago, never dangerous; major work to do.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 115,000gns yearling by Supremacy; half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Kairyu (including Group 3; RPR 109); 40-1, however, for maiden at Sandown (7f, good) and he finished sixth of seven after leading for 5f; modest form but he could have come on since that run and perhaps 6f will prove a better fit.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: E Kelly, J Woods, N Gallagher & Partner
Spotlight: 14,000euros foal, 30,000euros yearling; third foal; Space Blues half-brother to 5f winner Boyne Lady (RPR 74); dam twice-raced (60) half-sister to winners Centennial (1m4f Group 2) and Siren's Song (1m2f Listed), out of half-sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Croco Rouge; stable has some pretty useful 2yos and one was successful here on debut in May.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By No Nay Never; second foal; dam 5f/5.7f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 108), sister to winners Wild Majesty (French 5f Listed) and Mr Cool (6f), half-sister to useful US 5f-7f winner Big Handsome; newcomer to note.

RaceName: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £12,000 breeze-up 2yo; 33-1 for six-runner novice at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) two weeks ago, but made most and battled on for second behind an impressive winner; should improve and has place chance again at least.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner; career-best RPR when making all up the stands' rail at Lingfield (7f, good; Class 6) last month; creditable fourth at Goodwood since; could face competition for the lead this time.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time winner, including twice on Polytrack at Lingfield, and her first two runs of the campaign were encouraging; below that level on turf last time but she is the type to bounce back.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Her win came over 6f on slow turf but she has run well over C&D in the past and this return to AW could be beneficial; others appeal more for win purposes though.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 6f win here as a 2yo; her only handicap win came in a three-runner affair at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) last summer; she hasn't found her best form in three runs since leaving Alice Haynes and cheekpieces are now reached for.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise over C&D on debut (July 2024) and followed up at Wolverhampton that month; 0-11 in handicaps though and her best run this year came in a Class 6; others look stronger.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: M H Dixon & J L Rowsell
Spotlight: Four respectable efforts in handicaps following her maiden win at Leicester (1m, good to soft) last October; ran well when third here (1m) last month and she now drops into 0-70 company; wide draw not ideal for one who likes to race handily.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 7f turf win as a 2yo; promising seasonal return when second of ten at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in April but she took a backward step at Newbury 18 day ago; now tried in cheekpieces for the first time.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner; made all over 1m here in January and has run to a similar level in four runs since, including when blinkers replaced cheekpieces 25 days ago; creeping up the weights and there are other pace influences to deal with today.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Promise on AW, including C&D, as a 2yo; only midfield on handicap debut when last seen in December; could do better this year but she will need to.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: No improvement for either 7f or handicap company when fourth of five at Sandown (soft ground) when last seen ten months ago but perhaps underfoot conditions weren't to her liking; dropped 4lb and still has potential; Jack Callan's claim is a bonus; usual hood is discarded but she looks interesting.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to progress from her debut run (5f, AW) in three further starts for James Tate this spring; sold for 3,400gns last month; up in trip and hooded for this stable debut.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up at Wolverhampton and over C&D in her two starts this winter; not so good on slow turf three weeks ago but she moves into handicaps with potential.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Quite lightly raced since going up 11lb for a wide-margin win at Newmarket (6f, good) in May 2025, but a recent second on soft ground at Epsom (6f; cheekpieces refitted) was a useful effort at the weights and showed him to be back on track; should be in contention again.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Improved AW form over 6f last winter, but wasn't on his game when last seen on sand in April and he made it 0-11 on grass when beaten about 5l at Newmarket in May; may have his share of weight on balance of turf form.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has run all over the place for different yards and in good form for this one, finishing close up in a big 6f York race before winning at Thirsk (6f, good); had a very stiff task in the Wokingham at Royal Ascot where he did well to hang on to his lead for as long as he did on the wrong side of the track; this easy 6f will suit better; can go well.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Talented 2yo; soundly beaten on turf reappearance in 2025 and was kept to the AW for the rest of the year; probably helped by racing near the stand rail when making a winning return at Ascot (6f, good) in May, but still made a good impression and an early stumble counted against him at Chester since; should still have some improvement in him; thereabouts.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Course specialist; in prime form, having won at Bath before suffering a luckless run on this track, then a convincing winner here (also 5f, good to soft; acts well on good to firm) since; that trip seems to suit best though and he has more to prove off 6lb higher in a better race.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Five 6f wins in 2025, on AW and turf (good); now 14lb higher than his final run on grass last year and, although he faced difficult tasks in two Newmarket starts this spring, he might have run better in the second of them; needs to raise his turf game.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Three sprint wins in 2025 on extremes of going, including over C&D; excelled herself by going close in a 5f Listed race at Bath on return but she couldn't take advantage of her old mark back in a handicap next time and an 11lb higher mark contributed to an Epsom defeat (5f) since; still not ruled out back over 6f.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Both handicap wins have come at Chester (6f and 7f) and he looked to be coming to the boil over the extended 7f there in May, when having a luckless run; rare to see him in 6f turf handicaps these days, though, and has work to do.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Narrow 6f defeat at Chepstow last August but his next two runs at the trip were a failure and kept to shorter since, winning at Haydock (good to soft) in September; two respectable efforts at 5f this year (no aids latest) don't suggest this mark is ripe for plucking back at 6f, though.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won C&D novice then close up in a Group 3 at Glorious Goodwood (5f) last July before losing some momentum; gelded since and was never nearer than at the line on his Newmarket comeback (6f, good to firm) in May; open to improvement and no surprise to see him involved.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Multiple wins but he's won just once on turf in nearly two years and is suited by good or slower ground; two fair efforts of late (no help from the draw at Chester recently) but there may be other days.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: £42,500 yearling, £70,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; brother to 6f winner Tawasol (RPR 97); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 78), half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Tabdeed, out of maiden half-sister to Derby runner-up Walk In The Park; enough paper appeal to warrant a market check.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: £10,000 yearling, resold 2,400gns yearling; second foal; dam a maiden half-sister to a winner in Japan; would be a surprise winner on debut, with trainer 0-54 with 2yos in the last five seasons.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: 85,000euros foal, 95,000gns yearling; first foal of an unraced half-sister to a couple of useful winners; stable's 2yo winner this year won at the second attempt.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: 16,000euros foal, 30,000euros yearling, £42,000 breeze-up 2yo; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Hala Blue (6f AW 2yo; RPR 77) and Royal Status (Italian 1m-11.5f); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (93), half-sister to US stakes-placed 5f 2yo winner; stable still seeking a first 2yo win of the year.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
OwnerName: Mr And Mrs B V Sangster
Spotlight: 20-1 for his debut at Salisbury (6f, good) two weeks ago, weakening out of it in the closing stages; open to improvement and should last longer today.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: Dropped away to finish eighth of ten over C&D in April (8-1); gelded since; open to improvement but he is drawn widest and may prove vulnerable.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: Sent off a bigger price than Tumishi on Nottingham debut and finished just over 1l behind him in sixth but that may have been a different story had he seen any daylight when trying to launch a challenge; there again shouldn't be much between the pair this time.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: 7,500euros yearling, £50,000 breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-brother to Italian 1m winner Witty Indian; dam unplaced at 7f-1m3f (RPR 58), half-sister to 14.5f Group 2 winner Silk Sari, out of unraced half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Ibn Khaldun; should stay further in time but still worth a market check.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: Easy to back and never threatened on last week's debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm); of interest only if attracting support.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: 180,000gns breeze-up 2yo; shaped well when fourth (Primal sixth) at Nottingham on debut 26 days ago, leaving the impression he would have learned from the experience; while the three to beat him have all been beaten since, two were at Royal Ascot and the other was in a Listed race; interesting.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)
Spotlight: Slowly away and always in rear on last month's Goodwood debut (6f, good; 25-1); not easily recommended on the back of that.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins off higher marks last season, including C&D, and promising third at Bath this April on her stable/seasonal debut; however, her form has gone the wrong since and she needs to raise her game substantially.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: Races prominently (effective from the front) and he's having a cracking campaign, including when back to turf for first and second in tight finishes at Brighton on last two outings; back up another 3lb and didn't shine here earlier in career, but he needs plenty of respect now.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: Third run back with Tony Carroll when taking advantage of his reduced mark at Bath (5.7f) last month and he did it in good style; followed up under a 4lb penalty at Brighton (6f, again good to firm) one week later, leading in the final strides, so this 5f could be an issue but he's on a roll again.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner; no win anywhere since October 2024 but he's been placed this year on both his visits to Windsor (5f, good to firm); drawn wider this time but he's considered each-way.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
OwnerName: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd
Spotlight: Nothing like the force of old but he won at Lingfield in May and has been bang there at Newbury and Brighton (beaten a nose) on both starts since, all over about 5f on good to firm; should go well again.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: 1-1 at Windsor, thanks to a 6f handicap in 2024, and also scored at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) last June; overall strike-rate is 2-29 but usually thereabouts and each-way claims on this first start since leaving Ruth Carr.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: 6-35 for Stuart Williams but 0-11 on turf and, despite a falling mark, that's the obvious concern on this first start for new connections.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: 3-31 for Tim Easterby, including a Catterick win (5f, good) last August; second at Lingfield (6f, AW) on stable debut in December but not so good in her three runs since, so others have more pressing claims; some headgear (not used by current yard) returns.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: Placed in her first three starts over 6f/7f on turf/AW last summer, but has beaten a total of one rival in her four handicaps (some headgear in all four); this is her first go at 5f and a return to good to firm might help.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: 0-7 but a drop to 5.7f at Bath (good; unraced on firmer) last time produced a front-running second of 11 off today's mark; admittedly beaten 3l but he was clear at one stage and this first attempt at 5f is interesting.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: His last win was in April 2024, which is probably the bottom line; three wins and five seconds from 12 races at Windsor also catches the eye but he was a soundly beaten last at Leicester on reappearance three weeks ago.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden but keeps getting placed, last time in 7f handicap at Yarmouth (soft; acts on good to firm) 19 days ago on her only start for Ilka Gansera-Leveque; unraced over this short a trip but it's an interesting move.

RaceName: Distro Design Handicap
Spotlight: AW maiden winner as 2yo but 0-20 otherwise; went handicapping off a high mark and his 3yo season was a write-off; fortunes have turned somewhat off basement marks this term but he's more persuasive on AW.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Hugely progressive for his new stable at the start of the year, winning handicaps at Southwell (7f/1m) before completing his hat-trick at Newcastle (1m); beaten on turf twice since but he is 0-10 in that sphere as opposed to 5-8 on AW; drawn widest but still of some interest.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Four course wins over 7f; went close at that trip last month and a subsequent turf defeat isn't the run to judge him on; has had only one attempt at 1m and he appeared to stay; career best needed to win, though.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Thrown in on 2024 best but he missed 2025 and has yet to get going this time around; hopes appear to rest on the drop in class having a positive effect.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Seven-time AW winner for Darryll Holland, notably of a 7f handicap at Southwell in March 2025 when 5lb higher; well beaten in three runs for Jim Boyle and, although the last two were on turf, enough to prove even back on AW.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a modest effort at Newmarket but had been making steady progress beforehand; with just seven runs to her name it is not unreasonable to expect further progress as a 4yo; one to consider.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Seven AW wins, the latest on his seasonal return last month when getting the better of I'm Working On It (7f); midfield on turf latest; returning to AW is in his favour but all his wins have come at 7f (0-10 at 1m+) and he could prove vulnerable to stronger stayers today.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: His ability is not in question but he needs everything to fall right and has often flattered to deceive; stepping back up to 1m is worth a go but others look safer.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Ended his losing run at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in March and he did well to follow up over 7f one week later; not quite so good since but he has the ability to go well off this mark; headgear tried last time is now quickly dispensed with.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Back to winning ways off this mark at Lingfield (1m, AW; cheekpieces first time) in April; he hasn't built on that and others may have his measure.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two AW wins for former stable; latest turf run is not the race to judge him on but, even so, he comes here with plenty to prove.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a ready win in a small-field nursery at Newcastle (1m); better form when second of seven, beaten a short head, at Southwell in April; flopped on fast turf last month but he could resume his progress back on AW.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Beat a good horse when winning over C&D on debut; struggled in two turf runs since but the return to AW could see him tap back into that debut promise; blinkers are tried today after one run in cheekpieces.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 1m AW win for the Gosdens in November; not so good in two turf runs over further in April; sold for 28,000gns in May; new stable quick to drop him in trip and fit headgear.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Finished second on his first three starts (AW; around 1m); never a threat on handicap/turf debut (7.6f, soft) 17 days ago; back on AW but needs to prove this mark is in range.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Looked good in her 2yo win (1m, AW) for Paul Attwater and came from last to first when the 18-1 outsider of six on seasonal, stable and handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good) nine days ago, asserting in good style despite carrying her head a bit high; up 9lb but probably has more to give.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won two of his first four starts before missing the second half of last season; made an eye-catching reappearance from well off the pace this April and went mighty close when he reverted to front-running over that same Newbury 1m2f (good) on latest outing; tongue tied first time in the latter; 5lb rise sees claimer Mason Paetel recruited; leading player.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 3-40 overall; usually thereabouts on turf/AW, including this year, but latest start was an exception and he needs to bounce back from that Ripon run.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: S Mizon, H McAlpine & Winner Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Has some back-class but now 0-10 for this yard and the form he showed in initial outings last summer has not been repeated; edging down the weights but the jockey here on penultimate start reported that he hung badly; others are more convincing.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Pretty useful in light Flat career for James Ferguson last term, before hurdling for new yard didn't really work out; last seen in January and had wind surgery soon after; tongue-tie returns and he has cheekpieces first time, probably needing to better last year's form.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four
Spotlight: Maiden winner who is 0-13 in handicaps; has a reduced mark and competitive this term, including over C&D, but others are preferred for win purposes.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a breakthrough win at Nottingham (1m) and back on the scoresheet by a short head when making all at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) 11 days ago; back up 3lb (5lb claimer is recruited) and should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a Brighton maiden and 1m2f AW handicap at Lingfield; subsided rapidly to be last of six in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm; 33-1) and no show at Epsom (1m4f, soft; 100-1) in the Derby itself, but he returns to handicaps here on what looks a pretty good mark.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual Polytrack winner (1m2f/1m4f) last summer and was returning from 279 days off when sixth of 13 in a 0-90 contest here (1m4f) last month; that run may well have brought him on and he remains unexposed for a 5yo after just nine starts; high on list.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Four-time winner on Polytrack (1m/1m2f), but yet to click since returning for this yard in January, refusing to race on one occasion; has dropped a long way in the weights, but risky.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 3lb lower than when winning here (1m4f) in September 2024 and ran well to finish fourth of ten in a 0-105 contest over C&D (first-time blinkers, now removed) in January but a five-month break and high draw do pose questions.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Consistent mare whose three wins on the AW include two here (1m/1m4f); still on a fair mark and should again run her race.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Twice made all on turf (1m2f, good) last July but has been off for eight months and was well held in one previous go on the AW (Tapeta), while she remains 8lb above her last winning mark; market useful; stable also runs Crystal Mariner.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Not fired in two starts on turf since returning last month but he won twice over C&D last autumn and is just 1lb higher than for his latest success; worth a second look.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Five-race maiden who has finished out of the frame in two turf handicaps since returning last month, the latest when fitted with this headgear combination; another 2lb drop not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has endured a couple of lengthy absences in his seven-race career but showed the ability remains when successful in a first-time hood (now removed) at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) last month; half-brother to a couple of AW winners and shortlisted; stable also runs Mathematician.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 0-11 on the AW and 1-18 overall; down another 2lb and cheekpieces are given a go this time, but he still has stamina to prove over this far.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 15-8 favourite for his handicap debut/reappearance at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) in April but appeared to hang his chance away on the fast ground; brother to an AW winner and half-brother to another; a better performance this time would be no great surprise.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Placed a couple of times on turf last autumn but no win since September 2024 and still 2lb higher; has run with credit here, but likely he will need this after 252 days off.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won impressively over C&D for Roger Varian in September 2023 but not seen since; returns for his new yard off an 8lb higher mark and best watched after such a mammoth absence, unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Again no sign of the tongue-tie he wore for most wins; two runs this season were his first on turf since June 2024 and he needs to return to February's AW levels.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Pretty consistent since the cheekpieces returned in November and the three wins in that period include 11-runner race at Brighton (1m, good to firm) last time; back up another 2lb and has to be considered.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Easily best known for exploits at Bath, where he's been runner-up three times this season; 0-10 here, admittedly twice runner-up and also third of 15 in May (1m2f) but he may have to settle for another place return.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Lingfield (7.6f, good) last time saw him surge to prominence in his second handicap, coming second of 13 at 33-1; back up 2lb but very lightly raced and could have more to offer in the first-time headgear.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Started his career well but it's been poor from April 2024 onwards; has had only two races for current yard and now sports tongue-tie and cheekpieces, both for the first time; 30lb lower than his opening mark.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was fourth in three 1m/1m2f events last year and is edging down the weights; not so good with blinkers enlisted this term and a hood (tried once, she ran well) now joins them.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Got behind after a slow start at Bath (1m, firm) on penultimate start but still got to within a head of the winner; hampered there when the hood was absent next time; 2-26 overall but one to consider.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: 0-12; closest finish when second at Nottingham (1m, good) in April and perhaps 1m2f stretched her when she returned to turf for latest outing; player judged on best efforts.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins came in 2024 when trained by Charlie Johnston; has lost her form and is now with her third trainer since; pulled up here (bled from the nose) on penultimate start; first headgear today.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins on Lingfield AW but runner-up twice from six starts here; having run so poorly back on turf last time is the big stumbling block.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Huge prices for all four starts and no worthwhile form; latest was her handicap/seasonal debut over 11.4f here.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Modest sprint maiden as 2yo but 2025 did not go at all well; absent for a year; changed hands and left Andrew Martin; new yard had a winner on Friday.

RaceName: Colenko Fast Track Handicap
Spotlight: Several places last year for Sean Woods but none in six starts for this yard and he's now 0-18; off since February and goes beyond 7f for first time.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten a nose here last August (1m4f) off 2lb higher; disappointing run over hurdles five weeks ago, when a remote last of three, will need forgiving, but he has claims otherwise in a weak race going this far for the first time; back to blinkers from cheekpieces.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Handicap
Spotlight: Two course wins to his name from four starts; none of his six victories have come off a mark quite this high and he's lost his way since going in at Wolverhampton in March; needs a revival.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Handicap
